standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.domain: College and Career Readiness Anchor Standards for Language
standards.standard_text: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.domain: College and Career Readiness Anchor Standards for Language
standards.standard_text: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.domain: College and Career Readiness Anchor Standards for Language
standards.standard_text: Apply knowledge of language to understand how language functions in different contexts, to make effective choices for meaning or style, and to comprehend more fully when reading or listening.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.domain: College and Career Readiness Anchor Standards for Language
standards.standard_text: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases by using context clues, analyzing meaningful word parts, and consulting general and specialized reference materials, as appropriate.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.domain: College and Career Readiness Anchor Standards for Language
standards.standard_text: Demonstrate understanding of figurative language, word relationships, and nuances in word meanings.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.domain: College and Career Readiness Anchor Standards for Language
standards.standard_text: Acquire and use accurately a range of general academic and domain-specific words and phrases sufficient for reading, writing, speaking, and listening at the college and career readiness level; demonstrate independence in gathering vocabulary knowledge when encountering an unknown term important to comprehension or expression.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.domain: College and Career Readiness Anchor Standards for Reading
standards.standard_text: Read closely to determine what the text says explicitly and to make logical inferences from it; cite specific textual evidence when writing or speaking to support conclusions drawn from the text.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.domain: College and Career Readiness Anchor Standards for Reading
standards.cluster: Range of Reading and Level of Text Complexity
standards.standard_text: Read and comprehend complex literary and informational texts independently and proficiently.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.domain: College and Career Readiness Anchor Standards for Reading
standards.standard_text: Determine central ideas or themes of a text and analyze their development; summarize the key supporting details and ideas.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.domain: College and Career Readiness Anchor Standards for Reading
standards.standard_text: Analyze how and why individuals, events, and ideas develop and interact over the course of a text.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.domain: College and Career Readiness Anchor Standards for Reading
standards.standard_text: Interpret words and phrases as they are used in a text, including determining technical, connotative, and figurative meanings, and analyze how specific word choices shape meaning or tone.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.domain: College and Career Readiness Anchor Standards for Reading
standards.standard_text: Analyze the structure of texts, including how specific sentences, paragraphs, and larger portions of the text (e.g., a section, chapter, scene, or stanza) relate to each other and the whole.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.domain: College and Career Readiness Anchor Standards for Reading
standards.standard_text: Assess how point of view or purpose shapes the content and style of a text.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.domain: College and Career Readiness Anchor Standards for Reading
standards.standard_text: Integrate and evaluate content presented in diverse media and formats, including visually and quantitatively, as well as in words.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.domain: College and Career Readiness Anchor Standards for Reading
standards.standard_text: Delineate and evaluate the argument and specific claims in a text, including the validity of the reasoning as well as the relevance and sufficiency of the evidence.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.domain: College and Career Readiness Anchor Standards for Reading
standards.standard_text: Analyze how two or more texts address similar themes or topics in order to build knowledge or to compare the approaches the authors take.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.domain: College and Career Readiness Anchor Standards for Speaking and Listening
standards.standard_text: Prepare for and participate effectively in a range of conversations and collaborations with diverse partners, building on others' ideas and expressing their own clearly and persuasively.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.domain: College and Career Readiness Anchor Standards for Speaking and Listening
standards.standard_text: Integrate and evaluate information presented in diverse media and formats, including visually, quantitatively, and orally.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.domain: College and Career Readiness Anchor Standards for Speaking and Listening
standards.standard_text: Evaluate a speaker's point of view, reasoning, and use of evidence and rhetoric.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.domain: College and Career Readiness Anchor Standards for Speaking and Listening
standards.standard_text: Present information, findings, and supporting evidence such that listeners can follow the line of reasoning and the organization, development, and style are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.domain: College and Career Readiness Anchor Standards for Speaking and Listening
standards.standard_text: Make strategic use of digital media and visual displays of data to express information and enhance understanding of presentations.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.domain: College and Career Readiness Anchor Standards for Speaking and Listening
standards.standard_text: Adapt speech to a variety of contexts and communicative tasks, demonstrating command of formal English when indicated or appropriate.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.domain: College and Career Readiness Anchor Standards for Writing
standards.standard_text: Write arguments to support claims in an analysis of substantive topics or texts, using valid reasoning and relevant and sufficient evidence.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.domain: College and Career Readiness Anchor Standards for Writing
standards.standard_text: Write routinely over extended time frames (time for research, reflection, and revision) and shorter time frames (a single sitting or a day or two) for a range of tasks, purposes, and audiences.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.domain: College and Career Readiness Anchor Standards for Writing
standards.standard_text: Write informative/explanatory texts to examine and convey complex ideas and information clearly and accurately through the effective selection, organization, and analysis of content.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.domain: College and Career Readiness Anchor Standards for Writing
standards.standard_text: Write narratives to develop real or imagined experiences or events using effective technique, well-chosen details, and well-structured event sequences.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.domain: College and Career Readiness Anchor Standards for Writing
standards.standard_text: Produce clear and coherent writing in which the development, organization, and style are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.domain: College and Career Readiness Anchor Standards for Writing
standards.standard_text: Develop and strengthen writing as needed by planning, revising, editing, rewriting, or trying a new approach.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.domain: College and Career Readiness Anchor Standards for Writing
standards.standard_text: Use technology, including the Internet, to produce and publish writing and to interact and collaborate with others.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.domain: College and Career Readiness Anchor Standards for Writing
standards.cluster: Research to Build and Present Knowledge
standards.standard_text: Conduct short as well as more sustained research projects based on focused questions, demonstrating understanding of the subject under investigation.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.domain: College and Career Readiness Anchor Standards for Writing
standards.cluster: Research to Build and Present Knowledge
standards.standard_text: Gather relevant information from multiple print and digital sources, assess the credibility and accuracy of each source, and integrate the information while avoiding plagiarism.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.domain: College and Career Readiness Anchor Standards for Writing
standards.cluster: Research to Build and Present Knowledge
standards.standard_text: Draw evidence from literary or informational texts to support analysis, reflection, and research.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.standard_text: Follow agreed-upon rules for discussions (e.g., listening to others and taking turns speaking about the topics and texts under discussion).

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.standard_text: Continue a conversation through multiple exchanges.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.standard_text: Understand and follow one- and two-step oral directions.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.standard_text: Print many upper- and lowercase letters.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.standard_text: Use frequently occurring nouns and verbs.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.standard_text: Form regular plural nouns orally by adding /s/ or /es/ (e.g., dog, dogs; wish, wishes).

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.standard_text: Understand and use question words (interrogatives) (e.g., who, what, where, when, why, how).

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.standard_text: Use the most frequently occurring prepositions (e.g., to, from, in, out, on, off, for, of, by, with).

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.standard_text: Produce and expand complete sentences in shared language activities.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.standard_text: Capitalize the first word in a sentence and the pronoun I.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.standard_text: Recognize and name end punctuation.

standards.standard_text: Write a letter or letters for most consonant and short-vowel sounds (phonemes).

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.standard_text: Spell simple words phonetically, drawing on knowledge of sound-letter relationships.

standards.standard_text: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases based on kindergarten reading and content.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.standard_text: With guidance and support from adults, explore word relationships and nuances in word meanings.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.standard_text: Use words and phrases acquired through conversations, reading and being read to, and responding to texts.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.standard_text: Demonstrate basic knowledge of one-to-one letter-sound correspondences by producing the primary or many of the most frequent sound for each consonant.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.standard_text: Associate the long and short sounds with common spellings (graphemes) for the five major vowels. (Identify which letters represent the five major vowels [Aa, Ee, Ii, Oo, and Uu] and know the long and short sound of each vowel. More complex long vowel graphemes and spellings are targeted in the grade 1 phonics standards.)

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.standard_text: Read common high-frequency words by sight (e.g., the, of, to, you, she, my, is, are, do, does).

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.standard_text: Distinguish between similarly spelled words by identifying the sounds of the letters that differ.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.standard_text: Recognize and produce rhyming words.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.standard_text: Count, pronounce, blend, and segment syllables in spoken words.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.standard_text: Blend and segment onsets and rimes of single-syllable spoken words.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.standard_text: Isolate and pronounce the initial, medial vowel, and final sounds (phonemes) in three-phoneme (consonent-vowel-consonent, or CVC) words. (This does not include CVCs ending with /l/, /r/, or /x/.)

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.standard_text: Add or substitute individual sounds (phonemes) in simple, one-syllable words to make new words.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.standard_text: Blend two to three phonemes into recognizable words.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.standard_text: Follow words from left to right, top to bottom, and page by page.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.standard_text: Recognize that spoken words are represented in written language by specific sequences of letters.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.standard_text: Understand that words are separated by spaces in print.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.standard_text: Recognize and name all upper- and lowercase letters of the alphabet.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.standard_text: Demonstrate understanding of the organization and basic features of print.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.standard_text: Demonstrate understanding of spoken words, syllables, and sounds (phonemes).

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.standard_text: Speak audibly and express thoughts, feelings, and ideas clearly.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.standard_text: Use the most frequently occurring inflections and affixes (e.g., -ed, -s, re-, un-, pre-, -ful, -less) as a clue to the meaning of an unknown word.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.standard_text: Sort common objects into categories (e.g., shapes, foods) to gain a sense of the concepts the categories represent.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.standard_text: Demonstrate understanding of frequently occurring verbs and adjectives by relating them to their opposites (antonyms).

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.standard_text: Use frequently occurring adjectives.

standards.standard_text: Draw evidence from literary or informational texts to support analysis, reflection, and research.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.standard_text: Come to discussions prepared, having read and researched material under study; explicitly draw on that preparation by referring to evidence from texts and other research on the topic or issue to stimulate a thoughtful, well-reasoned exchange of ideas.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.standard_text: Work with peers to promote civil, democratic discussions and decision-making, set clear goals and deadlines, and establish individual roles as needed.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.standard_text: Propel conversations by posing and responding to questions that probe reasoning and evidence; ensure a hearing for a full range of positions on a topic or issue; clarify, verify, or challenge ideas and conclusions; and promote divergent and creative perspectives.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.standard_text: Respond thoughtfully to diverse perspectives; synthesize comments, claims, and evidence made on all sides of an issue; resolve contradictions when possible; and determine what additional information or research is required to deepen the investigation or complete the task.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.standard_text: Come to discussions prepared, having read and researched material under study; explicitly draw on that preparation by referring to evidence from texts and other research on the topic or issue to stimulate a thoughtful, well-reasoned exchange of ideas.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.standard_text: Work with peers to set rules for collegial discussions and decision-making (e.g., informal consensus, taking votes on key issues, presentation of alternate views), clear goals and deadlines, and individual roles as needed.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.standard_text: Propel conversations by posing and responding to questions that relate the current discussion to broader themes or larger ideas; actively incorporate others into the discussion; and clarify, verify, or challenge ideas and conclusions.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.standard_text: Respond thoughtfully to diverse perspectives, summarize points of agreement and disagreement, and, when warranted, qualify or justify their own views and understanding and make new connections in light of the evidence and reasoning presented.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.standard_text: Spell correctly.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.standard_text: Apply the understanding that usage is a matter of convention, can change over time, and is sometimes contested.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.standard_text: Resolve issues of complex or contested usage, consulting references (e.g., Merriam-Webster's Dictionary of English Usage, Garner's Modern American Usage) as needed.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.standard_text: Observe hyphenation conventions.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.standard_text: Use parallel structure.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.standard_text: Use various types of phrases (noun, verb, adjectival, adverbial, participial, prepositional, absolute) and clauses (independent, dependent; noun, relative, adverbial) to convey specific meanings and add variety and interest to writing or presentations.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.standard_text: Use a semicolon (and perhaps a conjunctive adverb) to link two or more closely related independent clauses.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.standard_text: Use a colon to introduce a list or quotation.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.standard_text: Spell correctly.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.standard_text: Analyze the use of text features (e.g., graphics, headers, captions) in public documents.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.standard_text: Analyze the use of text features (e.g., graphics, headers, captions) in functional workplace documents.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.standard_text: Vary syntax for effect, consulting references (e.g., Tufte's Artful Sentences) for guidance as needed; apply an understanding of syntax to the study of complex texts when reading.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.standard_text: Write and edit work so that it conforms to the guidelines in a style manual (e.g., MLA Handbook, Turabian's Manual for Writers) appropriate for the discipline and writing type.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.standard_text: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.standard_text: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.standard_text: Apply knowledge of language to understand how language functions in different contexts, to make effective choices for meaning or style, and to comprehend more fully when reading or listening.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.standard_text: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases based on grades 11—12 reading and content, choosing flexibly from a range of strategies.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.standard_text: Demonstrate understanding of figurative language, word relationships, and nuances in word meanings.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.standard_text: Acquire and use accurately general academic and domain-specific words and phrases, sufficient for reading, writing, speaking, and listening at the college and career readiness level; demonstrate independence in gathering vocabulary knowledge when considering a word or phrase important to comprehension or expression.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.standard_text: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.standard_text: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.standard_text: Apply knowledge of language to understand how language functions in different contexts, to make effective choices for meaning or style, and to comprehend more fully when reading or listening.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.standard_text: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases based on grades 9—10 reading and content, choosing flexibly from a range of strategies.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.standard_text: Demonstrate understanding of figurative language, word relationships, and nuances in word meanings.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.standard_text: Acquire and use accurately general academic and domain-specific words and phrases, sufficient for reading, writing, speaking, and listening at the college and career readiness level; demonstrate independence in gathering vocabulary knowledge when considering a word or phrase important to comprehension or expression.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.standard_text: Plan and deliver a reflective narrative that: explores the significance of a personal experience, event, or concern; uses sensory language to convey a vivid picture; includes appropriate narrative techniques (e.g., dialogue, pacing, description); and draws comparisons between the specific incident and broader themes. (11th or 12th grade)

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.standard_text: Plan and present an argument that: supports a precise claim; provides a logical sequence for claims, counterclaims, and evidence; uses rhetorical devices to support assertions (e.g., analogy, appeal to logic through reasoning, appeal to emotion or ethical belief); uses varied syntax to link major sections of the presentation to create cohesion and clarity; and provides a concluding statement that supports the argument presented. (11th or 12th grade)

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.standard_text: Plan and deliver an informative/explanatory presentation that: presents evidence in support of a thesis, conveys information from primary and secondary sources coherently, uses domain specific vocabulary, and provides a conclusion that summarizes the main points. (9th or 10th grade)

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.standard_text: Plan, memorize, and present a recitation (e.g., poem, selection from a speech or dramatic soliloquy) that: conveys the meaning of the selection and includes appropriate performance techniques (e.g., tone, rate, voice modulation) to achieve the desired aesthetic effect. (9th or 10th grade)

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.standard_text: Cite strong and thorough textual evidence to support analysis of what the text says explicitly as well as inferences drawn from the text, including determining where the text leaves matters uncertain.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.cluster: Range of Reading and Level of Text Complexity
standards.standard_text:
By the end of grade 11, read and comprehend literary nonfiction in the grades 11—CCR text complexity band proficiently, with scaffolding as needed at the high end of the range.
By the end of grade 12, read and comprehend literary nonfiction at the high end of the grades 11—CCR text complexity band independently and proficiently.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.standard_text: Determine two or more central ideas of a text and analyze their development over the course of the text, including how they interact and build on one another to provide a complex analysis; provide an objective summary of the text.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.standard_text: Analyze a complex set of ideas or sequence of events and explain how specific individuals, ideas, or events interact and develop over the course of the text.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.standard_text: Determine the meaning of words and phrases as they are used in a text, including figurative, connotative, and technical meanings; analyze how an author uses and refines the meaning of a key term or terms over the course of a text (e.g., how Madison defines faction in Federalist No. 10). (See grade 11–12 Language standards 4–6 for additional expectations.)

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.standard_text: Analyze and evaluate the effectiveness of the structure an author uses in his or her exposition or argument, including whether the structure makes points clear, convincing, and engaging.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.standard_text: Determine an author's point of view or purpose in a text in which the rhetoric is particularly effective, analyzing how style and content contribute to the power, persuasiveness, or beauty of the text.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.standard_text: Integrate and evaluate multiple sources of information presented in different media or formats (e.g., visually, quantitatively) as well as in words in order to address a question or solve a problem.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.standard_text: Delineate and evaluate the reasoning in seminal U.S. texts, including the application of constitutional principles and use of legal reasoning (e.g., in U.S. Supreme Court majority opinions and dissents) and the premises, purposes, and arguments in works of public advocacy (e.g., The Federalist, presidential addresses).

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.standard_text: Analyze seventeenth-, eighteenth-, and nineteenth-century foundational U.S. documents of historical and literary significance (including The Declaration of Independence, the Preamble to the Constitution, the Bill of Rights, and Lincoln's Second Inaugural Address) for their themes, purposes, and rhetorical features.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.standard_text: Cite strong and thorough textual evidence to support analysis of what the text says explicitly as well as inferences drawn from the text.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.cluster: Range of Reading and Level of Text Complexity
standards.standard_text:
By the end of grade 9, read and comprehend literary nonfiction in the grades 9—10 text complexity band proficiently, with scaffolding as needed at the high end of the range.
By the end of grade 10, read and comprehend literary nonfiction at the high end of the grades 9—10 text complexity band independently and proficiently.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.standard_text: Determine a central idea of a text and analyze its development over the course of the text, including how it emerges and is shaped and refined by specific details; provide an objective summary of the text.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.standard_text: Analyze how the author unfolds an analysis or series of ideas or events, including the order in which the points are made, how they are introduced and developed, and the connections that are drawn between them.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.standard_text: Determine the meaning of words and phrases as they are used in a text, including figurative, connotative, and technical meanings; analyze the cumulative impact of specific word choices on meaning and tone (e.g., how the language of a court opinion differs from that of a newspaper). (See grade 9–10 Language standards 4–6 for additional expectations.)

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.standard_text: Analyze in detail how an author's ideas or claims are developed and refined by particular sentences, paragraphs, or larger portions of a text (e.g., a section or chapter).

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.standard_text: Determine an author's point of view or purpose in a text and analyze how an author uses rhetoric to advance that point of view or purpose.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.standard_text: Analyze various accounts of a subject told in different mediums (e.g., a person's life story in both print and multimedia), determining which details are emphasized in each account.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.standard_text: Delineate and evaluate the argument and specific claims in a text, assessing whether the reasoning is valid and the evidence is relevant and sufficient; identify false statements and fallacious reasoning.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.standard_text: Analyze seminal U.S. documents of historical and literary significance (e.g., Washington's Farewell Address, the Gettysburg Address, Roosevelt's Four Freedoms speech, King's "Letter from Birmingham Jail"), including how they address related themes and concepts.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.domain: Reading Standards for Literacy in History/Social Studies 6—12
standards.standard_text: Cite specific textual evidence to support analysis of primary and secondary sources, connecting insights gained from specific details to an understanding of the text as a whole.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.domain: Reading Standards for Literacy in History/Social Studies 6—12
standards.cluster: Range of Reading and Level of Text Complexity
standards.standard_text: By the end of grade 12, read and comprehend history/social studies texts in the grades 11—CCR text complexity band independently and proficiently.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.domain: Reading Standards for Literacy in History/Social Studies 6—12
standards.standard_text: Determine the central ideas or information of a primary or secondary source; provide an accurate summary that makes clear the relationships among the key details and ideas.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.domain: Reading Standards for Literacy in History/Social Studies 6—12
standards.standard_text: Evaluate various explanations for actions or events and determine which explanation best accords with textual evidence, acknowledging where the text leaves matters uncertain.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.domain: Reading Standards for Literacy in History/Social Studies 6—12
standards.standard_text: Determine the meaning of words and phrases as they are used in a text, including analyzing how an author uses and refines the meaning of a key term over the course of a text (e.g., how Madison defines faction in Federalist No. 10).

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.domain: Reading Standards for Literacy in History/Social Studies 6—12
standards.standard_text: Analyze in detail how a complex primary source is structured, including how key sentences, paragraphs, and larger portions of the text contribute to the whole.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.domain: Reading Standards for Literacy in History/Social Studies 6—12
standards.standard_text: Evaluate authors' differing points of view on the same historical event or issue by assessing the authors' claims, reasoning, and evidence.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.domain: Reading Standards for Literacy in History/Social Studies 6—12
standards.standard_text: Integrate and evaluate multiple sources of information presented in diverse formats and media (e.g., visually, quantitatively, as well as in words) in order to address a question or solve a problem.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.domain: Reading Standards for Literacy in History/Social Studies 6—12
standards.standard_text: Evaluate an author's premises, claims, and evidence by corroborating or challenging them with other information.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.domain: Reading Standards for Literacy in History/Social Studies 6—12
standards.standard_text: Integrate information from diverse sources, both primary and secondary, into a coherent understanding of an idea or event, noting discrepancies among sources.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.domain: Reading Standards for Literacy in History/Social Studies 6—12
standards.standard_text: Cite specific textual evidence to support analysis of primary and secondary sources, attending to such features as the date and origin of the information.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.domain: Reading Standards for Literacy in History/Social Studies 6—12
standards.cluster: Range of Reading and Level of Text Complexity
standards.standard_text: By the end of grade 10, read and comprehend history/social studies texts in the grades 9—10 text complexity band independently and proficiently.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.domain: Reading Standards for Literacy in History/Social Studies 6—12
standards.standard_text: Determine the central ideas or information of a primary or secondary source; provide an accurate summary of how key events or ideas develop over the course of the text.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.domain: Reading Standards for Literacy in History/Social Studies 6—12
standards.standard_text: Analyze in detail a series of events described in a text; determine whether earlier events caused later ones or simply preceded them.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.domain: Reading Standards for Literacy in History/Social Studies 6—12
standards.standard_text: Determine the meaning of words and phrases as they are used in a text, including vocabulary describing political, social, or economic aspects of history/social studies.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.domain: Reading Standards for Literacy in History/Social Studies 6—12
standards.standard_text: Analyze how a text uses structure to emphasize key points or advance an explanation or analysis

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.domain: Reading Standards for Literacy in History/Social Studies 6—12
standards.standard_text: Compare the point of view of two or more authors for how they treat the same or similar topics, including which details they include and emphasize in their respective accounts.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.domain: Reading Standards for Literacy in History/Social Studies 6—12
standards.standard_text: Integrate quantitative or technical analysis (e.g., charts, research data) with qualitative analysis in print or digital text.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.domain: Reading Standards for Literacy in History/Social Studies 6—12
standards.standard_text: Assess the extent to which the reasoning and evidence in a text support the author's claims.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.domain: Reading Standards for Literacy in History/Social Studies 6—12
standards.standard_text: Compare and contrast treatments of the same topic in several primary and secondary sources.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.domain: Reading Standards for Literacy in Science and Technical Subjects 6—12
standards.standard_text: Cite specific textual evidence to support analysis of science and technical texts, attending to important distinctions the author makes and to any gaps or inconsistencies in the account.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.domain: Reading Standards for Literacy in Science and Technical Subjects 6—12
standards.cluster: Range of Reading and Level of Text Complexity
standards.standard_text: By the end of grade 12, read and comprehend science/technical texts in the grades 11—CCR text complexity band independently and proficiently.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.domain: Reading Standards for Literacy in Science and Technical Subjects 6—12
standards.standard_text: Determine the central ideas or conclusions of a text; summarize complex concepts, processes, or information presented in a text by paraphrasing them in simpler but still accurate terms.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.domain: Reading Standards for Literacy in Science and Technical Subjects 6—12
standards.standard_text: Follow precisely a complex multistep procedure when carrying out experiments, taking measurements, or performing technical tasks; analyze the specific results based on explanations in the text.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.domain: Reading Standards for Literacy in Science and Technical Subjects 6—12
standards.standard_text: Determine the meaning of symbols, key terms, and other domain-specific words and phrases as they are used in a specific scientific or technical context relevant to grades 11—12 texts and topics.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.domain: Reading Standards for Literacy in Science and Technical Subjects 6—12
standards.standard_text: Analyze how the text structures information or ideas into categories or hierarchies, demonstrating understanding of the information or ideas.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.domain: Reading Standards for Literacy in Science and Technical Subjects 6—12
standards.standard_text: Analyze the author's purpose in providing an explanation, describing a procedure, or discussing an experiment in a text, identifying important issues that remain unresolved.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.domain: Reading Standards for Literacy in Science and Technical Subjects 6—12
standards.standard_text: Integrate and evaluate multiple sources of information presented in diverse formats and media (e.g., quantitative data, video, multimedia) in order to address a question or solve a problem.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.domain: Reading Standards for Literacy in Science and Technical Subjects 6—12
standards.standard_text: Evaluate the hypotheses, data, analysis, and conclusions in a science or technical text, verifying the data when possible and corroborating or challenging conclusions with other sources of information.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.domain: Reading Standards for Literacy in Science and Technical Subjects 6—12
standards.standard_text: Synthesize information from a range of sources (e.g., texts, experiments, simulations) into a coherent understanding of a process, phenomenon, or concept, resolving conflicting information when possible.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.domain: Reading Standards for Literacy in Science and Technical Subjects 6—12
standards.standard_text: Cite specific textual evidence to support analysis of science and technical texts, attending to the precise details of explanations or descriptions.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.domain: Reading Standards for Literacy in Science and Technical Subjects 6—12
standards.cluster: Range of Reading and Level of Text Complexity
standards.standard_text: By the end of grade 10, read and comprehend science/technical texts in the grades 9—10 text complexity band independently and proficiently.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.domain: Reading Standards for Literacy in Science and Technical Subjects 6—12
standards.standard_text: Determine the central ideas or conclusions of a text; trace the text's explanation or depiction of a complex process, phenomenon, or concept; provide an accurate summary of the text.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.domain: Reading Standards for Literacy in Science and Technical Subjects 6—12
standards.standard_text: Follow precisely a complex multistep procedure when carrying out experiments, taking measurements, or performing technical tasks, attending to special cases or exceptions defined in the text.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.domain: Reading Standards for Literacy in Science and Technical Subjects 6—12
standards.standard_text: Determine the meaning of symbols, key terms, and other domain-specific words and phrases as they are used in a specific scientific or technical context relevant to grades 9—10 texts and topics.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.domain: Reading Standards for Literacy in Science and Technical Subjects 6—12
standards.standard_text: Analyze the structure of the relationships among concepts in a text, including relationships among key terms (e.g., force, friction, reaction force, energy).

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.domain: Reading Standards for Literacy in Science and Technical Subjects 6—12
standards.standard_text: Analyze the author's purpose in providing an explanation, describing a procedure, or discussing an experiment in a text, defining the question the author seeks to address.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.domain: Reading Standards for Literacy in Science and Technical Subjects 6—12
standards.standard_text: Translate quantitative or technical information expressed in words in a text into visual form (e.g., a table or chart) and translate information expressed visually or mathematically (e.g., in an equation) into words.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.domain: Reading Standards for Literacy in Science and Technical Subjects 6—12
standards.standard_text: Assess the extent to which the reasoning and evidence in a text support the author's claim or a recommendation for solving a scientific or technical problem.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.domain: Reading Standards for Literacy in Science and Technical Subjects 6—12
standards.standard_text: Compare and contrast findings presented in a text to those from other sources (including their own experiments), noting when the findings support or contradict previous explanations or accounts.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.standard_text: Cite strong and thorough textual evidence to support analysis of what the text says explicitly as well as inferences drawn from the text, including determining where the text leaves matters uncertain.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.cluster: Range of Reading and Level of Text Complexity
standards.standard_text:
By the end of grade 11, read and comprehend literature, including stories, dramas, and poems, in the grades 11—CCR text complexity band proficiently, with scaffolding as needed at the high end of the range.
By the end of grade 12, read and comprehend literature, including stories, dramas, and poems, at the high end of the grades 11—CCR text complexity band independently and proficiently.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.standard_text: Determine two or more themes or central ideas of a text and analyze their development over the course of the text, including how they interact and build on one another to produce a complex account; provide an objective summary of the text.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.standard_text: Analyze the impact of the author's choices regarding how to develop and relate elements of a story or drama (e.g., where a story is set, how the action is ordered, how the characters/archetypes are introduced and developed).

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.standard_text: Determine the meaning of words and phrases as they are used in the text, including figurative and connotative meanings; analyze the impact of specific word choices on meaning and tone, including words with multiple meanings or language that is particularly fresh, engaging, or beautiful. (Include Shakespeare as well as other authors.) (See grade 11–12 Language standards 4–6 for additional expectations.)

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.standard_text: Analyze how an author's choices concerning how to structure specific parts of a text (e.g., the choice of where to begin or end a story, the choice to provide a comedic or tragic resolution) contribute to its overall structure and meaning as well as its aesthetic impact.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.standard_text: Analyze a case in which grasping point of view requires distinguishing what is directly stated in a text from what is really meant (e.g., satire, sarcasm, irony, or understatement).

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.standard_text: Analyze multiple interpretations of a story, drama, or poem (e.g., recorded or live production of a play or recorded novel or poetry), evaluating how each version interprets the source text. (Include at least one play by Shakespeare and one play by an American dramatist.)

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.standard_text: Demonstrate knowledge of eighteenth-, nineteenth- and early-twentieth-century foundational works of American literature, including how two or more texts from the same period treat similar themes or topics.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.standard_text: Cite strong and thorough textual evidence to support analysis of what the text says explicitly as well as inferences drawn from the text.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.cluster: Range of Reading and Level of Text Complexity
standards.standard_text:
By the end of grade 9, read and comprehend literature, including stories, dramas, and poems, in the grades 9—10 text complexity band proficiently, with scaffolding as needed at the high end of the range.
By the end of grade 10, read and comprehend literature, including stories, dramas, and poems, at the high end of the grades 9—10 text complexity band independently and proficiently.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.standard_text: Determine a theme or central idea of a text and analyze in detail its development over the course of the text, including how it emerges and is shaped and refined by specific details; provide an objective summary of the text.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.standard_text: Analyze how complex characters (e.g., those with multiple or conflicting motivations) develop over the course of a text, interact with other characters, and advance the plot or develop the theme.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.standard_text: Determine the meaning of words and phrases as they are used in the text, including figurative and connotative meanings; analyze the cumulative impact of specific word choices on meaning and tone (e.g., how the language evokes a sense of time and place; how it sets a formal or informal tone). (See grade 9–10 Language standards 4–6 for additional expectations.)

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.standard_text: Analyze how an author's choices concerning how to structure a text, order events within it (e.g., parallel plots), and manipulate time (e.g., pacing, flashbacks) create such effects as mystery, tension, or surprise.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.standard_text: Analyze a particular point of view or cultural experience reflected in a work of literature from outside the United States, drawing on a wide reading of world literature.

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.standard_text: Analyze the representation of a subject or a key scene in two different artistic mediums, including what is emphasized or absent in each treatment (e.g., Auden's "Musée des Beaux Arts" and Breughel's Landscape with the Fall of Icarus).

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.standard_text: Analyze how an author draws on and transforms source material in a specific work (e.g., how Shakespeare treats a theme or topic from Ovid or the Bible or how a later author draws on a play by Shakespeare).

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.domain: Research to Build and Present Knowledge
standards.cluster: Research to Build and Present Knowledge
standards.standard_text: Apply grades 11—12 Reading standards to literature (e.g., "Demonstrate knowledge of eighteenth-, nineteenth- and early-twentieth-century foundational works of American literature, including how two or more texts from the same period treat similar themes or topics").

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.domain: Research to Build and Present Knowledge
standards.cluster: Research to Build and Present Knowledge
standards.standard_text: Apply grades 11—12 Reading standards to literary nonfiction (e.g., "Delineate and evaluate the reasoning in seminal U.S. texts, including the application of constitutional principles and use of legal reasoning [e.g., in U.S. Supreme Court Case majority opinions and dissents] and the premises, purposes, and arguments in works of public advocacy [e.g., The Federalist, presidential addresses]").

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.domain: Research to Build and Present Knowledge
standards.cluster: Research to Build and Present Knowledge
standards.standard_text: Apply grades 9—10 Reading standards to literature (e.g., "Analyze how an author draws on and transforms source material in a specific work [e.g., how Shakespeare treats a theme or topic from Ovid or the Bible or how a later author draws on a play by Shakespeare]").

standards.subject: ELA-Literacy
standards.domain: Research to Build and Present Knowledge
standards.cluster: Research to Build and Present Knowledge

standards.subject: Math
standards.cluster: Know number names and the count sequence.
standards.standard_text: Count to 100 by ones and by tens.

standards.subject: Math
standards.cluster: Know number names and the count sequence.
standards.standard_text: Count forward beginning from a given number within the known sequence (instead of having to begin at 1).

standards.subject: Math
standards.cluster: Know number names and the count sequence.
standards.standard_text: Write numbers from 0 to 20. Represent a number of objects with a written numeral 0-20 (with 0 representing a count of no objects).

standards.subject: Math
standards.cluster: Count to tell the number of objects.
standards.standard_text: Understand the relationship between numbers and quantities; connect counting to cardinality.

standards.subject: Math
standards.cluster: Count to tell the number of objects.
standards.standard_text: When counting objects, say the number names in the standard order, pairing each object with one and only one number name and each number name with one and only one object.

standards.subject: Math
standards.cluster: Count to tell the number of objects.
standards.standard_text: Understand that the last number name said tells the number of objects counted. The number of objects is the same regardless of their arrangement or the order in which they were counted.

standards.subject: Math
standards.cluster: Count to tell the number of objects.
standards.standard_text: Understand that each successive number name refers to a quantity that is one larger.

standards.subject: Math
standards.cluster: Count to tell the number of objects.
standards.standard_text: Count to answer "how many?" questions about as many as 20 things arranged in a line, a rectangular array, or a circle, or as many as 10 things in a scattered configuration; given a number from 1—20, count out that many objects.

standards.subject: Math
standards.standard_text: Identify whether the number of objects in one group is greater than, less than, or equal to the number of objects in another group, e.g., by using matching and counting strategies.

standards.subject: Math
standards.standard_text: Compare two numbers between 1 and 10 presented as written numerals.

standards.subject: Math
standards.cluster: Identify and describe shapes (squares, circles, triangles, rectangles, hexagons, cubes, cones, cylinders, and spheres).
standards.standard_text: Describe objects in the environment using names of shapes, and describe the relative positions of these objects using terms such as above, below, beside, in front of, behind, and next to.

standards.subject: Math
standards.cluster: Identify and describe shapes (squares, circles, triangles, rectangles, hexagons, cubes, cones, cylinders, and spheres).
standards.standard_text: Correctly name shapes regardless of their orientations or overall size.

standards.subject: Math
standards.cluster: Identify and describe shapes (squares, circles, triangles, rectangles, hexagons, cubes, cones, cylinders, and spheres).
standards.standard_text: Identify shapes as two-dimensional (lying in a plane, "flat") or three-dimensional ("solid").

standards.subject: Math
standards.cluster: Analyze, compare, create, and compose shapes.
standards.standard_text: Analyze and compare two- and three-dimensional shapes, in different sizes and orientations, using informal language to describe their similarities, differences, parts (e.g., number of sides and vertices/"corners") and other attributes (e.g., having sides of equal length).

standards.subject: Math
standards.cluster: Analyze, compare, create, and compose shapes.
standards.standard_text: Model shapes in the world by building shapes from components (e.g., sticks and clay balls) and drawing shapes.

standards.subject: Math
standards.cluster: Analyze, compare, create, and compose shapes.
standards.standard_text: Compose simple shapes to form larger shapes.

standards.subject: Math
standards.standard_text: Describe measurable attributes of objects, such as length or weight. Describe several measurable attributes of a single object.

standards.subject: Math
standards.standard_text: Directly compare two objects with a measurable attribute in common, to see which object has "more of"/"less of" the attribute, and describe the difference.

standards.subject: Math
standards.cluster: Classify objects and count the number of objects in each category.
standards.standard_text: Classify objects into given categories; count the numbers of objects in each category and sort the categories by count.

standards.subject: Math
standards.domain: Number and Operations in Base Ten
standards.cluster: Work with numbers 11—19 to gain foundations for place value.
standards.standard_text: Compose and decompose numbers from 11 to 19 into ten ones and some further ones, e.g., by using objects or drawings, and record each composition or decomposition by a drawing or equation (e.g., 18 = 10 + 8); understand that these numbers are composed of ten ones and one, two, three, four, five, six, seven, eight, or nine ones.

standards.subject: Math
standards.cluster: Understand addition as putting together and adding to, and understand subtraction as taking apart and taking from.
standards.standard_text: Represent addition and subtraction with objects, fingers, mental images, drawings, sounds (e.g., claps), acting out situations, verbal explanations, expressions, or equations.

standards.subject: Math
standards.cluster: Understand addition as putting together and adding to, and understand subtraction as taking apart and taking from.
standards.standard_text: Solve addition and subtraction word problems, and add and subtract within 10, e.g., by using objects or drawings to represent the problem.

standards.subject: Math
standards.cluster: Understand addition as putting together and adding to, and understand subtraction as taking apart and taking from.
standards.standard_text: Decompose numbers less than or equal to 10 into pairs in more than one way, e.g., by using objects or drawings, and record each decomposition by a drawing or equation (e.g., 5 = 2 + 3 and 5 = 4 + 1).

standards.subject: Math
standards.cluster: Understand addition as putting together and adding to, and understand subtraction as taking apart and taking from.
standards.standard_text: For any number from 1 to 9, find the number that makes 10 when added to the given number, e.g., by using objects or drawings, and record the answer with a drawing or equation.

standards.subject: Math
standards.cluster: Understand addition as putting together and adding to, and understand subtraction as taking apart and taking from.
standards.standard_text: Fluently add and subtract within 5.

standards.subject: Math
standards.cluster: Reason with shapes and their attributes.
standards.standard_text: Distinguish between defining attributes (e.g., triangles are closed and three-sided) versus non-defining attributes (e.g., color, orientation, overall size); build and draw shapes to possess defining attributes.

standards.subject: Math
standards.cluster: Reason with shapes and their attributes.
standards.standard_text: Compose two-dimensional shapes (rectangles, squares, trapezoids, triangles, half-circles, and quarter-circles) or three-dimensional shapes (cubes, right rectangular prisms, right circular cones, and right circular cylinders) to create a composite shape, and compose new shapes from the composite shape.

standards.subject: Math
standards.cluster: Reason with shapes and their attributes.
standards.standard_text: Partition circles and rectangles into two and four equal shares, describe the shares using the words halves, fourths, and quarters, and use the phrases half of, fourth of, and quarter of. Describe the whole as two of, or four of the shares. Understand for these examples that decomposing into more equal shares creates smaller shares.

standards.subject: Math
standards.cluster: Measure lengths indirectly and by iterating length units.
standards.standard_text: Order three objects by length; compare the lengths of two objects indirectly by using a third object.

standards.subject: Math
standards.cluster: Measure lengths indirectly and by iterating length units.
standards.standard_text: Express the length of an object as a whole number of length units, by laying multiple copies of a shorter object (the length unit) end to end; understand that the length measurement of an object is the number of same-size length units that span it with no gaps or overlaps.

standards.subject: Math
standards.standard_text: Tell and write time in hours and half-hours using analog and digital clocks.

standards.subject: Math
standards.standard_text: Organize, represent, and interpret data with up to three categories; ask and answer questions about the total number of data points, how many in each category, and how many more or less are in one category than in another.

standards.subject: Math
standards.domain: Number and Operations in Base Ten
standards.standard_text: Count to 120, starting at any number less than 120. In this range, read and write numerals and represent a number of objects with a written numeral.

standards.subject: Math
standards.domain: Number and Operations in Base Ten
standards.standard_text: Understand that the two digits of a two-digit number represent amounts of tens and ones. Understand the following as special cases:

standards.subject: Math
standards.domain: Number and Operations in Base Ten
standards.standard_text: 10 can be thought of as a bundle of ten ones — called a "ten."

standards.subject: Math
standards.domain: Number and Operations in Base Ten
standards.standard_text: The numbers from 11 to 19 are composed of a ten and one, two, three, four, five, six, seven, eight, or nine ones.

standards.subject: Math
standards.domain: Number and Operations in Base Ten
standards.standard_text: The numbers 10, 20, 30, 40, 50, 60, 70, 80, 90 refer to one, two, three, four, five, six, seven, eight, or nine tens (and 0 ones).

standards.subject: Math
standards.domain: Number and Operations in Base Ten
standards.standard_text: Compare two two-digit numbers based on meanings of the tens and ones digits, recording the results of comparisons with the symbols >, =, and <.

standards.subject: Math
standards.domain: Number and Operations in Base Ten
standards.cluster: Use place value understanding and properties of operations to add and subtract.
standards.standard_text: Add within 100, including adding a two-digit number and a one-digit number, and adding a two-digit number and a multiple of 10, using concrete models or drawings and strategies based on place value, properties of operations, and/or the relationship between addition and subtraction; relate the strategy to a written method and explain the reasoning used. Understand that in adding two-digit numbers, one adds tens and tens, ones and ones; and sometimes it is necessary to compose a ten.

standards.subject: Math
standards.domain: Number and Operations in Base Ten
standards.cluster: Use place value understanding and properties of operations to add and subtract.
standards.standard_text: Given a two-digit number, mentally find 10 more or 10 less than the number, without having to count; explain the reasoning used.

standards.subject: Math
standards.domain: Number and Operations in Base Ten
standards.cluster: Use place value understanding and properties of operations to add and subtract.
standards.standard_text: Subtract multiples of 10 in the range 10-90 from multiples of 10 in the range 10-90 (positive or zero differences), using concrete models or drawings and strategies based on place value, properties of operations, and/or the relationship between addition and subtraction; relate the strategy to a written method and explain the reasoning used.

standards.subject: Math
standards.cluster: Represent and solve problems involving addition and subtraction.
standards.standard_text: Use addition and subtraction within 20 to solve word problems involving situations of adding to, taking from, putting together, taking apart, and comparing, with unknowns in all positions, e.g., by using objects, drawings, and equations with a symbol for the unknown number to represent the problem.

standards.subject: Math
standards.cluster: Represent and solve problems involving addition and subtraction.
standards.standard_text: Solve word problems that call for addition of three whole numbers whose sum is less than or equal to 20, e.g., by using objects, drawings, and equations with a symbol for the unknown number to represent the problem.

standards.subject: Math
standards.cluster: Understand and apply properties of operations and the relationship between addition and subtraction.
standards.standard_text: Apply properties of operations as strategies to add and subtract.

standards.subject: Math
standards.cluster: Understand and apply properties of operations and the relationship between addition and subtraction.
standards.standard_text: Understand subtraction as an unknown-addend problem.

standards.subject: Math
standards.standard_text: Relate counting to addition and subtraction (e.g., by counting on 2 to add 2).

standards.subject: Math
standards.standard_text: Add and subtract within 20, demonstrating fluency for addition and subtraction within 10. Use strategies such as counting on; making ten (e.g., 8 + 6 = 8 + 2 + 4 = 10 + 4 = 14); decomposing a number leading to a ten (e.g., 13 - 4 = 13 - 3 - 1 = 10 - 1 = 9); using the relationship between addition and subtraction (e.g., knowing that 8 + 4 = 12, one knows 12 - 8 = 4); and creating equivalent but easier or known sums (e.g., adding 6 + 7 by creating the known equivalent 6 + 6 + 1 = 12 + 1 = 13).

standards.subject: Math
standards.cluster: Work with addition and subtraction equations.
standards.standard_text: Understand the meaning of the equal sign, and determine if equations involving addition and subtraction are true or false.

standards.subject: Math
standards.cluster: Work with addition and subtraction equations.
standards.standard_text: Determine the unknown whole number in an addition or subtraction equation relating three whole numbers.

standards.subject: Math
standards.cluster: Reason with shapes and their attributes.
standards.standard_text: Recognize and draw shapes having specified attributes, such as a given number of angles or a given number of equal faces. Identify triangles, quadrilaterals, pentagons, hexagons, and cubes.

standards.subject: Math
standards.cluster: Reason with shapes and their attributes.
standards.standard_text: Partition a rectangle into rows and columns of same-size squares and count to find the total number of them.

standards.subject: Math
standards.cluster: Reason with shapes and their attributes.
standards.standard_text: Partition circles and rectangles into two, three, or four equal shares, describe the shares using the words halves, thirds, half of, a third of, etc., and describe the whole as two halves, three thirds, four fourths. Recognize that equal shares of identical wholes need not have the same shape.

standards.subject: Math
standards.cluster: Measure and estimate lengths in standard units.
standards.standard_text: Measure the length of an object by selecting and using appropriate tools such as rulers, yardsticks, meter sticks, and measuring tapes.

standards.subject: Math
standards.standard_text: Draw a picture graph and a bar graph (with single-unit scale) to represent a data set with up to four categories. Solve simple put-together, take-apart, and compare problems using information presented in a bar graph.

standards.subject: Math
standards.cluster: Measure and estimate lengths in standard units.
standards.standard_text: Measure the length of an object twice, using length units of different lengths for the two measurements; describe how the two measurements relate to the size of the unit chosen.

standards.subject: Math
standards.cluster: Measure and estimate lengths in standard units.
standards.standard_text: Estimate lengths using units of inches, feet, centimeters, and meters.

standards.subject: Math
standards.cluster: Measure and estimate lengths in standard units.
standards.standard_text: Measure to determine how much longer one object is than another, expressing the length difference in terms of a standard length unit.

standards.subject: Math
standards.cluster: Relate addition and subtraction to length.
standards.standard_text: Use addition and subtraction within 100 to solve word problems involving lengths that are given in the same units, e.g., by using drawings (such as drawings of rulers) and equations with a symbol for the unknown number to represent the problem.

standards.subject: Math
standards.cluster: Relate addition and subtraction to length.
standards.standard_text: Represent whole numbers as lengths from 0 on a number line diagram with equally spaced points corresponding to the numbers 0, 1, 2, …, and represent whole-number sums and differences within 100 on a number line diagram.

standards.subject: Math
standards.standard_text: Tell and write time from analog and digital clocks to the nearest five minutes, using a.m. and p.m. Know relationships of time (e.g., minutes in an hour, days in a month, weeks in a year).

standards.subject: Math
standards.standard_text: Solve word problems involving dollar bills, quarters, dimes, nickels, and pennies, using $ and ¢ symbols appropriately.

standards.subject: Math
standards.standard_text: Generate measurement data by measuring lengths of several objects to the nearest whole unit, or by making repeated measurements of the same object. Show the measurements by making a line plot, where the horizontal scale is marked off in whole-number units.

standards.subject: Math
standards.domain: Number and Operations in Base Ten
standards.standard_text: Understand that the three digits of a three-digit number represent amounts of hundreds, tens, and ones; e.g., 706 equals 7 hundreds, 0 tens, and 6 ones. Understand the following as special cases:

standards.subject: Math
standards.domain: Number and Operations in Base Ten
standards.standard_text: 100 can be thought of as a bundle of ten tens — called a "hundred."

standards.subject: Math
standards.domain: Number and Operations in Base Ten
standards.standard_text: The numbers 100, 200, 300, 400, 500, 600, 700, 800, 900 refer to one, two, three, four, five, six, seven, eight, or nine hundreds (and 0 tens and 0 ones).

standards.subject: Math
standards.domain: Number and Operations in Base Ten
standards.standard_text: Count within 1000; skip-count by 2s, 5s, 10s, and 100s.

standards.subject: Math
standards.domain: Number and Operations in Base Ten
standards.standard_text: Read and write numbers to 1000 using base-ten numerals, number names, and expanded form.

standards.subject: Math
standards.domain: Number and Operations in Base Ten
standards.standard_text: Compare two three-digit numbers based on meanings of the hundreds, tens, and ones digits, using >, =, and < symbols to record the results of comparisons.

standards.subject: Math
standards.domain: Number and Operations in Base Ten
standards.cluster: Use place value understanding and properties of operations to add and subtract.
standards.standard_text: Fluently add and subtract within 100 using strategies based on place value, properties of operations, and/or the relationship between addition and subtraction.

standards.subject: Math
standards.domain: Number and Operations in Base Ten
standards.cluster: Use place value understanding and properties of operations to add and subtract.
standards.standard_text: Add up to four two-digit numbers using strategies based on place value and properties of operations.

standards.subject: Math
standards.domain: Number and Operations in Base Ten
standards.cluster: Use place value understanding and properties of operations to add and subtract.
standards.standard_text: Add and subtract within 1000, using concrete models or drawings and strategies based on place value, properties of operations, and/or the relationship between addition and subtraction; relate the strategy to a written method. Understand that in adding or subtracting three-digit numbers, one adds or subtracts hundreds and hundreds, tens and tens, ones and ones; and sometimes it is necessary to compose or decompose tens or hundreds.

standards.subject: Math
standards.domain: Number and Operations in Base Ten
standards.cluster: Use place value understanding and properties of operations to add and subtract.
standards.standard_text: Use estimation strategies to make reasonable estimates in problem solving.

standards.subject: Math
standards.domain: Number and Operations in Base Ten
standards.cluster: Use place value understanding and properties of operations to add and subtract.
standards.standard_text: Mentally add 10 or 100 to a given number 100—900, and mentally subtract 10 or 100 from a given number 100—900.

standards.subject: Math
standards.domain: Number and Operations in Base Ten
standards.cluster: Use place value understanding and properties of operations to add and subtract.
standards.standard_text: Explain why addition and subtraction strategies work, using place value and the properties of operations.

standards.subject: Math
standards.cluster: Represent and solve problems involving addition and subtraction.
standards.standard_text: Use addition and subtraction within 100 to solve one- and two-step word problems involving situations of adding to, taking from, putting together, taking apart, and comparing, with unknowns in all positions, e.g., by using drawings and equations with a symbol for the unknown number to represent the problem.

standards.subject: Math
standards.standard_text: Fluently add and subtract within 20 using mental strategies. By end of Grade 2, know from memory all sums of two one-digit numbers.

standards.subject: Math
standards.cluster: Work with equal groups of objects to gain foundations for multiplication.
standards.standard_text: Determine whether a group of objects (up to 20) has an odd or even number of members, e.g., by pairing objects or counting them by 2s; write an equation to express an even number as a sum of two equal addends.

standards.subject: Math
standards.cluster: Work with equal groups of objects to gain foundations for multiplication.
standards.standard_text: Use addition to find the total number of objects arranged in rectangular arrays with up to 5 rows and up to 5 columns; write an equation to express the total as a sum of equal addends.

standards.subject: Math
standards.cluster: Reason with shapes and their attributes.
standards.standard_text: Understand that shapes in different categories (e.g., rhombuses, rectangles, and others) may share attributes (e.g., having four sides), and that the shared attributes can define a larger category (e.g., quadrilaterals). Recognize rhombuses, rectangles, and squares as examples of quadrilaterals, and draw examples of quadrilaterals that do not belong to any of these subcategories.

standards.subject: Math
standards.cluster: Reason with shapes and their attributes.
standards.standard_text: Partition shapes into parts with equal areas. Express the area of each part as a unit fraction of the whole.

standards.subject: Math
standards.cluster: Solve problems involving measurement and estimation of intervals of time, liquid volumes, and masses of objects.
standards.standard_text: Tell and write time to the nearest minute and measure time intervals in minutes. Solve word problems involving addition and subtraction of time intervals in minutes, e.g., by representing the problem on a number line diagram.

standards.subject: Math
standards.cluster: Solve problems involving measurement and estimation of intervals of time, liquid volumes, and masses of objects.
standards.standard_text: Measure and estimate liquid volumes and masses of objects using standard units of grams (g), kilograms (kg), and liters (l). Add, subtract, multiply, or divide to solve one-step word problems involving masses or volumes that are given in the same units, e.g., by using drawings (such as a beaker with a measurement scale) to represent the problem.

standards.subject: Math
standards.standard_text: Draw a scaled picture graph and a scaled bar graph to represent a data set with several categories. Solve one- and two-step "how many more" and "how many less" problems using information presented in scaled bar graphs.

standards.subject: Math
standards.standard_text: Generate measurement data by measuring lengths using rulers marked with halves and fourths of an inch. Show the data by making a line plot, where the horizontal scale is marked off in appropriate units— whole numbers, halves, or quarters.

standards.subject: Math
standards.cluster: Geometric measurement: understand concepts of area and relate area to multiplication and to addition.
standards.standard_text: Recognize area as an attribute of plane figures and understand concepts of area measurement.

standards.subject: Math
standards.cluster: Geometric measurement: understand concepts of area and relate area to multiplication and to addition.
standards.standard_text: A square with side length 1 unit, called "a unit square," is said to have "one square unit" of area, and can be used to measure area.

standards.subject: Math
standards.cluster: Geometric measurement: understand concepts of area and relate area to multiplication and to addition.
standards.standard_text: A plane figure which can be covered without gaps or overlaps by n unit squares is said to have an area of n square units.

standards.subject: Math
standards.cluster: Geometric measurement: understand concepts of area and relate area to multiplication and to addition.
standards.standard_text: Measure areas by counting unit squares (square cm, square m, square in, square ft, and improvised units).

standards.subject: Math
standards.cluster: Geometric measurement: understand concepts of area and relate area to multiplication and to addition.
standards.standard_text: Relate area to the operations of multiplication and addition.

standards.subject: Math
standards.cluster: Geometric measurement: understand concepts of area and relate area to multiplication and to addition.
standards.standard_text: Find the area of a rectangle with whole-number side lengths by tiling it, and show that the area is the same as would be found by multiplying the side lengths.

standards.subject: Math
standards.cluster: Geometric measurement: understand concepts of area and relate area to multiplication and to addition.
standards.standard_text: Multiply side lengths to find areas of rectangles with whole-number side lengths in the context of solving real world and mathematical problems, and represent whole-number products as rectangular areas in mathematical reasoning.

standards.subject: Math
standards.cluster: Geometric measurement: understand concepts of area and relate area to multiplication and to addition.
standards.standard_text: Use tiling to show in a concrete case that the area of a rectangle with whole-number side lengths a and b + c is the sum of a × b and a × c. Use area models to represent the distributive property in mathematical reasoning.

standards.subject: Math
standards.cluster: Geometric measurement: understand concepts of area and relate area to multiplication and to addition.
standards.standard_text: Recognize area as additive. Find areas of rectilinear figures by decomposing them into non-overlapping rectangles and adding the areas of the non-overlapping parts, applying this technique to solve real world problems.

standards.subject: Math
standards.cluster: Geometric measurement: recognize perimeter as an attribute of plane figures and distinguish between linear and area measures.
standards.standard_text: Solve real world and mathematical problems involving perimeters of polygons, including finding the perimeter given the side lengths, finding an unknown side length, and exhibiting rectangles with the same perimeter and different areas or with the same area and different perimeters.

standards.subject: Math
standards.domain: Number and Operations in Base Ten
standards.cluster: Use place value understanding and properties of operations to perform multi-digit arithmetic.
standards.standard_text: Use place value understanding to round whole numbers to the nearest 10 or 100.

standards.subject: Math
standards.domain: Number and Operations in Base Ten
standards.cluster: Use place value understanding and properties of operations to perform multi-digit arithmetic.
standards.standard_text: Fluently add and subtract within 1000 using strategies and algorithms based on place value, properties of operations, and/or the relationship between addition and subtraction.

standards.subject: Math
standards.domain: Number and Operations in Base Ten
standards.cluster: Use place value understanding and properties of operations to perform multi-digit arithmetic.
standards.standard_text: Multiply one-digit whole numbers by multiples of 10 in the range 10—90 (e.g., 9 × 80, 5 × 60) using strategies based on place value and properties of operations.

standards.subject: Math
standards.cluster: Develop understanding of fractions as numbers.
standards.standard_text: Understand a fraction 1/b as the quantity formed by 1 part when a whole is partitioned into b equal parts; understand a fraction a/b as the quantity formed by a parts of size 1/b.

standards.subject: Math
standards.cluster: Develop understanding of fractions as numbers.
standards.standard_text: Understand a fraction as a number on the number line; represent fractions on a number line diagram.

standards.subject: Math
standards.cluster: Develop understanding of fractions as numbers.
